hero.title: Multi-AI Rules for Consistent Coding
hero.description: One ruleset for Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Antigravity, Q, and Kiro. Consistent AI-assisted coding across all your tools.

problem.title: The Problem with AI Coding Today
problem.subtitle: Every AI tool has its own rules format. Your team wastes time maintaining duplicate configurations.
problem.pain1Desc: Each tool needs its own config — .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, .codex — with no shared standard.
problem.pain2Desc: Copy-paste the same coding rules across multiple tools every time you update them.
problem.pain3Desc: AI generates code without structured TDD, SOLID principles, or coverage requirements.
problem.pain4Desc: Different workflows per tool means no shared specialist knowledge or consistent methodology.

solution.title: One Ruleset. All Tools.
solution.subtitle: Codingbuddy gives your AI assistants consistent rules, specialist agents, and structured workflows.
solution.benefit1Desc: One ruleset automatically applied to Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Antigravity, Q, and Kiro.
solution.benefit2Desc: Focused expertise for architecture, security, testing, performance, accessibility, and more.
solution.benefit3Desc: PLAN → ACT → EVAL cycle ensures consistent quality across all your AI-assisted development.
solution.benefit4Desc: TDD, SOLID principles, and 90%+ test coverage enforced as standard practice.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Codingbuddy
faq.q1: What AI tools does Codingbuddy support?
faq.a1: Codingbuddy supports Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot (Codex), Antigravity (Gemini), Amazon Q, and Kiro. One ruleset works across all these tools via the MCP server protocol.
faq.q2: How does the PLAN/ACT/EVAL workflow work?
faq.a2: PLAN mode designs your implementation approach with TDD. ACT mode executes the changes. EVAL mode reviews code quality and suggests improvements. You can also use AUTO mode for autonomous cycling until quality targets are met.
faq.q3: Do I need to configure each AI tool separately?
faq.a3: No. Codingbuddy uses a single .ai-rules directory as the source of truth. Each AI tool reads from this shared ruleset through its adapter, so you only maintain one configuration.
faq.q4: Is Codingbuddy free and open source?
faq.a4: Yes. Codingbuddy is fully open source under the MIT license. You can use it freely in personal and commercial projects.
faq.q5: Can I customize the rules and agents?
faq.a5: Absolutely. You can create custom rules in the .ai-rules directory, define your own specialist agents, and adjust quality standards to match your team's workflow.
faq.a6: Specialist agents are 35 focused AI personas with expertise in specific areas like architecture review, security auditing, test strategy, performance optimization, and accessibility. They provide deep, domain-specific guidance.
